title: In Vitro Synthesized mRNA Transfection to Perkinsus marinus
author: Yoshihisa Hirakawa
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.vcme2u6

steps.instruction: We created the plasmid vector (T7-SL-eGFP) that the egfp gene with a 5′ UTR of the P. marinus TPT2 gene and a 3′ UTR of the P. marinus MOE gene was inserted in pSP72 (Promega). The 5′ UTR contains the spliced leader sequence (ACCGTAGCCATCTTGGCTCAAG) found in P. marinus nuclear genes. The construct of T7-SL-eGFP can be obtained from T7-SL-eGFP.pdf

mRNA with a 5′ m 7 G cap and 3′ poly(A)-tail was synthesized by mMESSAGE mMACHINE T7 Transcription Kit (Ambion) with the linearized plasmid of T7-SL-eGFP (it was digested by Pvu II). The synthesized mRNA was treated by TURBO DNase, and collected by ethanol precipitation according to the manufacturer’s instructions. The mRNA concentration was calculated by Qubit 3.0 (Thermo Life Technologies).

P. marinus cells (5×10 7 log-phase trophozoites) were collected by a brief centrifugation (3,000 g × 5 min) and the pellet was resuspended by 100 µL solution 2 supplied by Basic Parasite Nucleofector Kit 2 (Lonza). Synthesized mRNA was added to the solution in different concentration (1, 5, 10, or 20 µg). Electroporation was performed by Amaxa Nucleofector II (Lonza) with the D-023 program, and then the cells were transferred to 2 mL fresh medium (ATCC medium 1886) in a 6-well plate.
After 24 to 48 h, transfected cells were observed under an inverted fluorescence microscope with a GFP filter.
